_note: Boltz-2 BINARY co-folding (AF3-class) of denovo_401 into NR4A{3,1,2} LBD — independent pose/pocket cross-check of the docked pose used by the ABFE. Key metric = protein<->ligand pair_chains_iptm[0][1] (pocket-placement confidence) and ligand_iptm. Higher = more confident placement.

CRBN+lenalidomide_CONTROL.verdict: PASS — Boltz confidently seats a known in-distribution ligand

honest_interpretation: Control PASSES (0.78 interface, 0.99 ligand_iptm) -> pipeline valid.

All 3 NR4A binary co-folds have LOW ligand-interface confidence (pair_iptm 0.23-0.32 vs 0.78 control). Protein FOLD is confident (chain ptm 0.91-0.96); LIGAND PLACEMENT is not. This is exactly the pre-registered cryptic-pocket + orphan-receptor + de-novo-ligand failure mode: no ligand-bound NR4A structures exist in training data.

Cross-paralogue ordering does NOT favor NR4A3 -> by both metrics NR4A2 > NR4A1 > NR4A3, i.e. NR4A3 is LOWEST-confidence (opposite of the FEP selectivity), BUT all three are close and in the low-confidence regime -> within noise, not a real anti-selectivity signal.

CONCLUSION: the independent co-folder does NOT corroborate the FEP selectivity. It is in its known-unreliable regime here, so this is NOT a refutation of the FEP (a different, physics-based method on the metad-opened pocket) — but it is NOT independent support either. Net: the structure/pose axis remains the load-bearing uncertainty, and THIS breadth technique cannot resolve it.
